review_text:
Overall was a great value option if you want to stay at a cabin. I saw some reviews which were raised a few concerns but none of those were issues I faced whilst staying there.

Everything was clean within the cabins and public bathrooms were mostly good, apart from some other members forgetting to flush toilets or keeping the sink running (male)

Kitchen worked fine. Don't expect a 5 star cooking experience but was able to cook relatively easily without issue.

Showering without hot water was fine, but remember to bring a dollar if you can't handle cold water.

Only minor inconviences were that the cabin gets hot throughout the day, easily fixed with the solid ceiling fan and the fact that you'll probably be in town during the day. Some bug windows requiring some repairs and limited outlets to charge stuff.

Other than that everything was great, beds were comfortable, great curtains, lots of amenities you can expect from an accommodation like this all for a reasonable price of $125pn for the cheapest cabin

I highly recommended accommodation as long as you're aware of the limitations of going for the value/shared option and slightly further away from the Jervis Bay area.

From the moment we arrived the hosts were warm, welcoming, and incredibly helpful, making sure we had everything we needed. You can tell they genuinely care about creating a memorable experience for their guests.||The cabin was clean, spacious and beautifully maintained, with an easy to use BBQ on the terrace. The whole site was incredibly peaceful and full of birds and wildlife. Its also perfectly located, just a short drive from the amazing beaches. ||If you’re looking for a relaxing nature getaway with privacy, beauty, and convenience, Hidden Campsite is absolutely perfect!

Loved every moment at Hidden Creek, nothing but good vibes surrounded by Mother Nature in a beautiful setting. Highly recommend, would love to come back, hopefully will. Here with my 13 year old daughter and friends in the cabins, made some great memories.

Location is convenient, so close to Huskisson. Check in smoothly in the office, Tracey was so nice to give all tourist information around the area. The cabin is cozy and clean. The campsite is very quiet during night time. Parking just next to my cabin.
A streetlight right above the tent pitch, extremely loud neighbours, lunatics starting a smoky fire at 7:00 AM. Over rated and quite expensive campsite. You have to pay for a hot shower.

The cabins are perfectly located in the woods, but very close to huskissons. Coming by car is recommended, because going to the shops and/or national parks is more easily. The service was very good, the owners are super nice and helpful! They gave us great advice about things to do in and around the area. The cabin was spacious and big, which was super convenient. The only thing is that there is not an oven, which I thought I read about in the descriptions, but I'm not entirely sure. We loved to stay here and would absolutely recommend this to anyone!
It was a great, cosy little cabin situation in a nice bush area.  Reasonable prices, friendly staff, lovely animals (peacocks and ducks).
The place is very peaceful, clean and perfect for a camping vibe.
The route is convenient, the staff is friendly and helpful, the room is very clean, the surrounding atmosphere is quiet, and it is close to the beautiful beach

Be prepared to be greeted by a beautiful peacock as soon as you enter the premises |A thousand trees with just as much native wildlife. Countless possums, kangaroos, parrots, a wombat and even a couple of gliders.|Our cabin accommodation was spotlessly clean and inviting, then proved to be just as comfortable.|There is a small bat ramp in the camping area which is ok for a small tinny to be launched |The staff are extremely friendly and simply can't do enough to make you feel welcome |We only stayed for a weekend but will now be returning for a longer holiday

What a relaxing few days after the hectic time of Christmas. Sitting by the campfire alongside the creek, listening to the sounds of the birds and trees. |Visited by wallabies and being with nature. Kayaking down the creek, so surreal. |This campsite is a must. Just one with nature. |A local band playing in Huskisson and enjoying the spectacular white Sandy beaches and swimming the warm ocean waters.

Another amazing weekend spent at Hidden Creek Campsite. The tranquility of the campsite is perfect after a day exploring the many white sandy beaches. Jervis Bay is a picturesque gem with turquoise waters and crisp white sand and definitely the place to escape the busy city life.

The best campsite we have stayed at on our travels from Cairns to Melbourne! So pretty and peaceful. |Very friendly staff who provided a list of places to stop on the rest of the way to Melbourne. |Price was also very good compared to some of the worse, touristy spots we have stayed at costing much nore. Will definitely be back :))
